CROSS DEFENDANT JAY BLAKESBERG PHOTOGRAPHY, INC.'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT (HRG SET PER AGREEMENT PARTIES)
Off Calendar Feb-20-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. JUSTIN TRAUBEN, Esq. of Singh, Singh & Trauben, LLP (Tel: 310-856-9705) for Plaintiff;
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing Re: CROSS DEFENDANT JAY BLAKESBERG PHOTOGRAPHY, INC.'S DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT.

Matter not argued/contested. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Jay Blakesberg Photography, Inc. (JBP) and Jay Blakesberg's demurrer to Jim Marshall Photography LLC's (JMP) cross-complaint (CC) is overruled.
The CC pleads that "JMP owns the entire collection of photographs taken by world famous photographer and photojournalist Jim Marshall who died in 2010." (Id. at 2:7-8.) It is further pled that JMP and JBP entered into an oral agreement for JBP to act as licensing agent for orders of photos placed by clients of JBP and/or JMP. (Id. at 3:1-18.) To fulfill client orders, JBP was to produce, for JMP, images to be stored by the latter, including in jpeg files. (Id.) JMP does not plead a copyright claim; the word appears nowhere in the CC. As to claims the CC does assert, they suffice for the pleading stage:
Breach of oral agreement on a theory that "JBP breached the agreement by deleting and destroying the jpeg files." (CC 3:9-4:2; CACI 303, 304.)
Conversion on a theory that: "Despite" JMP's "exclusive ownership of the jpeg files," cross-defendants "converted the same by deleting or destroying said files." (CC 4:3-21; CACI 2100.)
Negligence on a theory that cross-defendants "as custodians of the jpeg files owed a duty of care to" JMP "to preserve the files." (CC 4:22-5:7; CACI 400.) Contract and tort theories may be pled alternatively. (Adams v. Paul (1995) 11 Cal.4th 583, 593.)
Breach of fiduciary duty against JBP on an agency theory. (CC 5:8-6:7; CACI 4100, 4101; Cleveland v. Johnson (2012) 209 Cal.App.4th 1315, 1339.) As to Blakesberg, a demurrer does not lie to part of a cause of action; if any part is properly pled, the demurrer is overruled. (Elder v. Pacific Bell Telephone Co. (2012) 205 Cal.App.4th 841, 856 n.14.)
Business and Professions Code Section 17200 and common law unfair competition. (CC 6:8-7:12.) JMP and JBP are pled to be competitors and equitable relief is sought. (Id. at 2:11-13; 6:18-20; 7:24-8:7.)
Accounting. (CC 7:13-23.) The claim need not be pled with particularity.
The CC's allegations could eventually prove to be unfounded, but no matter how unlikely or improbable, they must be accepted as true at the pleading stage. (Del E. Webb Corp. v. Structural Materials Corp. (1981) 123 Cal.App.3d 593, 604.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-31-2024 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. JUSTIN TRAUBEN, Esq. of Singh, Singh & Trauben, LLP (Tel: 310-856-9705) for Plaintiff;
No other appearances made in this matter.

Hearing RE: CROSS DEFENDANT JAY BLAKESBERG PHOTOGRAPHY, INC.'S DEMURRER to CROSS COMPLAINT

Matter discussed. The Court adopts its tentative ruling to take the matter of calendar for failure to provide courtesy copies of the opposition papers. The Court permits the parties after meeting and conferring to select a new hearing date on or after February 14, 2024. The Court instructs the parties to contact the Clerk of the Court to place the matter on calendar.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not Reported.(302/RBU)
